info.name: SMSPool API
info.description: This is the Postman directory for SMSPool in order to simplify your API needs.

item.name: Informative endpoints
Retrieve country success rates per service

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve success per country for SMSPool requests. It sends an HTTP POST request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/request/success_rate.

Please use either the service ID, or the service name as service param.

This HTTP POST request is used to retrieve the balance for a specific SMSPool. The request is sent to the URL https://api.smspool.net/request/balance.

Request Parameters

This request require the `key` param which is your API key

Response

The response to this request will have a status code of 200 if the request is successful. The response body will be in JSON format and will include a balance field. However, the last execution of this request returned an empty string for the balance field.

Please note that the actual value of the balance field may vary depending on the user and its configuration.

This API endpoint is used to retrieve suggested countries for SMS pool requests. It sends an HTTP POST request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/request/suggested_countries.

Please use either the service ID, or the service name as service param.

formdata.description: Service retrieved from ''Service list" endpoint
Country retrieved from ''Country list" endpoint
Return array format

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve suggested pools for SMSPool requests. It sends an HTTP POST request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/pool/retrieve_valid.

Please use either the service ID, or the service name as service param.

formdata.description: Service retrieved from ''Service list" endpoint
Country retrieved from ''Country list" endpoint
Return array format
Service retrieved from ''Service list" endpoint
Country retrieved from ''Country list" endpoint
Return array format

request.description: This API endpoint is used to retrieve the full country list for SMSPool requests. It sends an HTTP GET request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/country/retrieve_all.

formdata.description: Optional parameter to request services per country

request.description: This API endpoint is used to retrieve the full service list for SMSPool requests. It sends an HTTP GET request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/service/retrieve_all.
This API endpoint is used to retrieve a list of our active pools for SMSPool requests. It sends an HTTP POST request to the URL https://api.smspool.net/pool/retrieve_all.

item.description: All informative endpoints used in order to request extra information that were not categorized by the previous folders.

request.description: You may run into ratelimits while using this endpoint for high rate requests, we recommend using /request/active instead as it will list all your orders at the same time.

The recommended endpoint to retrieve your current active orders, this will display all your orders at the same time. We recommend using this endpoint to iterate through your active orders to retrieve your SMS messages.

Cancel your SMS order

Responses

| Success | {"success":1} | | Fail | {"success":0} |

response.name: 200 - order cancelled
400 - order cannot be cancelled yet

request.description:
Cancel your SMS order

Responses

| Success | {"success":1} | | Fail | {"success":0} |

Clear your previous SMS cache to allocate previous numbers again

Responses

| Success | {"success":1} |
| Fail | {"success":0} |
This endpoint will forcefully activate an SMS to set it available for future resends, please keep in mind that these orders are NOT eligible for a refund after activating them.

response.name: 200 - activate SMS
400 - incorrect pool

request.description: This endpoint will reactivate an order, please keep in mind that these orders are NOT eligible for a refund after activating them.

response.name: 200 - order reactivated

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to archive a request in the SMS Pool system. The HTTP POST request should be sent to https://api.smspool.net/request/archive.

Request Parameters

The `key` parameter is required for this endpoint, this is your API key.

Response

The response for this API endpoint will have a status code of 200, indicating a successful request. The response body will be in JSON format and will contain the following properties:

  • success (integer): A flag indicating the success of the request. A value of 0 indicates failure.
  • message (string): An optional message providing additional information about the request. In this case, the message is empty.

Please note that the response may contain additional properties not mentioned here, but they can be ignored for the purpose of this documentation.
Check if the order can be resent or not.

response.name: 400 - resend not available

request.description:
Resend

Resend the order. Keep in mind that some pools will have a charge per resend. You can see whether the resend will have a charge using the "Check Resend" endpoint

Responses

| Success | {"success":1,"message":"Number has been requested again","resend":0} |
| Fail | {"success":0,"message":"Phonenumber could not be requested again, try later again.","resend":0} |

response.name: 400 - missing orderid
400 - no initial sms
400 - resend not available Copy

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve one-time SMS stock in the SMSPool system. The HTTP POST request should be sent to https://api.smspool.net/sms/stock.

Request Parameters

The `key` parameter is required for this endpoint, this is your API key.

Response

The response for this API endpoint will have a status code of 200, indicating a successful request. The response body will be in JSON format and will contain the following properties:

  • success (integer): A flag indicating the success of the request. A value of 0 indicates failure.

  • message (string): An optional message providing additional information about the request. In this case, the message is empty.

Please note that the response may contain additional properties not mentioned here, but they can be ignored for the purpose of this documentation.

Service retrieved from ''Service list" endpoint (optional)
Pool retrieved from ''Pool list" endpoint (optional)

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve one-time SMS stock in the SMSPool system. The HTTP POST request should be sent to https://api.smspool.net/sms/stock.

Request Parameters

The `key` parameter is required for this endpoint, this is your API key.

Response

The response for this API endpoint will have a status code of 200, indicating a successful request. The response body will be in JSON format and will contain the following properties:

  • success (integer): A flag indicating the success of the request. A value of 0 indicates failure.

  • message (string): An optional message providing additional information about the request. In this case, the message is empty.

Please note that the response may contain additional properties not mentioned here, but they can be ignored for the purpose of this documentation.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve the history of SMS requests. It is an HTTP POST request that should be made to the following URL: https://api.smspool.net/request/history.

The request requires the `key` parameter which is your API key.

The response returned by the API is a JSON array. Each element in the array represents a historical SMS request and contains the following properties:

  • cost: The cost of the SMS request.
  • order_code: The order code associated with the SMS request.
  • phonenumber: The phone number to which the SMS was sent.
  • code: The code associated with the SMS request.
  • full_code: The full code associated with the SMS request.
  • short_name: The short name associated with the SMS request.
  • service: The service associated with the SMS request.
  • status: The status of the SMS request.
  • pool: The pool associated with the SMS request.
  • timestamp: The timestamp of the SMS request.
  • completed_on: The completion date of the SMS request.
  • expiry: The expiry time of the SMS request.
  • time_left: The time left for the SMS request to expire.

Please note that the values for the properties are not provided in the response example, so they may be empty or null.

The last execution of this API request returned a response with a status code of 200, indicating a successful execution. The response body contained an array of SMS request history objects.

Please refer to the API documentation for more details on how to use this endpoint and interpret the response.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve preorders from the SMS Pool system. It is an HTTP POST request sent to the URL https://api.smspool.net/preorder/retrieve.

Request Parameters

This endpoint requires the `key` parameter which is your API key.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to perform a paid lookup for a phone number. The request is sent via an HTTP POST method to the following URL: https://api.smspool.net/carrier/paid_lookup

{
  "success": true,
  "phonenumber": "",
  "country": "",
  "carrier": "",
  "carrier_type": "",
  "charge": 0
}

The success field indicates whether the lookup was successful or not. The phonenumber field represents the phone number for which the lookup was performed. The country field indicates the country associated with the phone number. The carrier field provides the name of the carrier associated with the phone number. The carrier_type field specifies the type of carrier. The charge field represents the cost charged for the lookup operation.

Please note that specific values for phonenumber, country, carrier, and carrier_type were not provided in the response, and the charge field had a value of 0.

To use this endpoint, send an HTTP POST request to the provided URL with the necessary parameters. The request should include the phone number for which you want to perform the lookup. The response will contain the details of the carrier and associated information.

Make sure to handle any errors that may occur during the lookup process and check the success field in the response to determine the outcome of the operation.

item.description:
Introduction to the eSIM API usage. Here's a step-to-step process on how to streamline your eSIM purchases:

  1. Retrieve the country list from View eSIM countries

  2. Retrieve the plans per country using the countryCode

  3. Create your first purchase using the Purchase eSIM

  4. Retrieve the transactionId from Purchase eSIM

  5. Use the View eSIM profile endpoint to retrieve the QR code, or the access URL in order to install the eSIM or view remaining data.

item.name: Voucher

formdata.description: The amount per promocode

item.description: Please keep in mind that this endpoint is only accessible for users that have special permissions to generate and maintain vouchers.
